MEMORANDUM OF UNDERSTANDING RELATING TO THE AIR SERVICES

AGREEMENT BETWEEN THE FRENCH REPUBLIC AND THE UNITED ARAB EMIRATES

Parls, 16 September 2004

Following their meeting of 9-10 February 2004, delegations representing the Aeronautical

Authorities of the French Republic and the Aeronautical Authorities of the United Arab

Emirates met in Paris on 16 September 2004, to discuss matters of common interest related to the bilateral Air Services Agreement signed between the two countries in Paris on 9

September 1991 (hereafter referred to as the "Agreement").

The discussions were held in the friendly and cordial atmosphere that characterises the good relationship between the two countries.

The list of delegations is attached as Annex A.

Both delegations addressed the following issues:

1)

Designation of airlines

a) Articles 4 and 5 of the Agreement

Both delegations agreed to recommend to their respective governments that article 4.4 and 5.1. a of the existing Agreement be replaced by the clauses as attached in Annex B.

The adoption of these designation and revocation clauses is without prejudice to the numbe of airlines which can be designated and to the frequency entitlements agreed between bott

Parties.

Bahrain and/or their nationals.

b) designation on route Abu Dhabi - Paris

Both delegations agreed that, notwithstanding the provisions of the CMOU of 30 October

1986. the designation of Etihad Airlines would be possible on the route Abu Dhabi-Paris and the French delegation agreed with this designation in corformity with articles 4 and 5 of

Il)

Capacity and frequencies

Both Parties agreed that the designated airlines of each contracting Party shall be allowed to operate, without any restriction on the aircraft type used :

• - a) on the Dubai-Paris sector (by each of Emirates Airline and the airline designated by

France):

• up to twelve (12) weekly frequencies as from IATA Summer Season 2005

• up to fourteen (14) weekly frequencies as from IATA Summer Season 200€

• -b) on the Dubai-Nice sector (by each of Emirates Airline and the airline designated by

France):

• up to three (3) weekly frequencies;

thos.

22/89/04 15:19

Pg: 3/9

• -c) on the Abu Dhabi-Paris/Lyons/Nice sectors:

• up to ten (10) weekly frequencies as from IATA Winter Season 2004/2005.

Accordingly, delegations agreed to remove the restriction stipulated in the MOU date 19

September 2001 in respect of the maximum number of frequencies that can be operated by a designated airline between the territories of the two countries on any given city pair.

Furthermore both Parties agreed to resume talks on the issue of additional frequency entitlement on the above sectors within two years in relation to market demand between the two countries.

川）

Code sharing operations

Both delegations agreed that in operating or holding out the authorised services on the agreed routes any designated airline of one Party may enter into co-operative arrangements such as blocked-space, code-sharing or leasing arrangements, with :

i)

її)

an airline or airlines of either Party:

an airline or airlines of a third country, provided that all airlines in such arrangements (a) hold the appropriate authority and (b) meet the requirements normally applied to such arrangements.

Notwithstanding the provisions of the Agreement, for the application of the code-sharing arrangements:

• the designated airline(s) of each Party may change gauge at any point from one aircraft to one or several aircraft;

- the marketing frequencies shall not be counted against the frequency entitlements of each Party.

IV) Route Schedule

In reference to the Route Schedule annexed to the Agreement, the French delegation nominated Amsterdam as one of the three additional intermediate points to be used by the designated airline(s) of the Republic of France.

V)

Intermodal transportation

In connection with international air transportation, designated airlines of each Party shall be permitted to hold out passenger services under their own name, through co-operative arrangements with surface transportation providers holding the appropriate authority to provide such surface transportation to and from any points in the territories of the Parties.

Surface transportation providers shall not be subject to laws and regulations governing air transportation on the sole basis that such surface transportation is held out by an airline under its own name. Such intermodal services may be offered at a single through price for the air and surface transportation combined, provided that passengers are not misled as to the facts concerning such transportation.

:

Surface transportation providers have the discretion to decide whether to enter into cooperative arrangements. In deciding on any particular arrangement, surface transportation providers may consider, among other things, consumer interests and technical, economic.

VI) 5th freedom traffic rights

Both delegations agreed to defer discussions on this issue until the next round of bilateral consultations.

Provisions of Annex B shall be confirmed through the diplomatic channel.

Done at Paris on 16 September 2004, in duplicate both texts being equally authentic.

For the delegation

For the delegation of the French Republic

of the United Arab Emirates

Philippe DUMONT

Mohamed Yahya AL SUWEIDI
